title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Execution Trace: Certificate Name Validation
summary: A service enables TLS without certificate-name validation, chooses HTTP after reading encrypted application bytes, allows unsafe versions, and treats encryption as authorization and content integrity proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the certificate name validation increment in TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Certificate Name Validation · Theory Certificate Name Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5792 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTPS on certificate identity, TLS policy, ALPN, and explicit early-data review; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then truncate one content octet and classify incomplete framing without connection reuse. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
